factors.name: Mega-cap Tech Earnings Momentum
factors.summary: SoftBank surged >11% on AI investment optimism; Lenovo jumped 13% on AI-driven earnings; AMD ramping production. Hyperscaler AI capex narrative remains intact despite macro headwinds.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.confidence_note: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed Chair on Friday; rising hawkish Fed tone lifting USD per Reuters. Mixed signals on rate trajectory but tone tilting restrictive.
factors.summary: Trump administration swearing in Warsh as Fed Chair signals potential policy shift. Market pricing rising odds of hawkish Fed stance, which typically compresses long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Sensitivity
factors.summary: Japan core CPI hit 4-year low (1.4% vs 1.7% expected); softer global inflation data (Australia jobs weak, Eurozone PMIs sliding into contraction). Lower inflation → potential for lower real yields, but Fed hawkish tilt may offset this benefit.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD at 6-week high (~99.25 DXY) per FXStreet; EUR/USD subdued at 1.1610 on hawkish Fed bets. Strong dollar pressures international revenue for NDX-heavy multinational tech firms (~45% of NVDA, MSFT revenue offshore).

factors.name: Oil & Risk-On Sentiment
factors.summary: WTI below $97, Brent capped ~$81-100 on US-Iran ceasefire progress, but Iran uranium stockpile stance complicates deal. Risk appetite modestly improved but energy shock inflation risks remain, limiting upside for equities.

factors.name: Semiconductor & AI Supply Chain
factors.summary: TSMC, ASML, Samsung demand remains robust; Perpetua Resources secured $2.9B US EIB loan for critical minerals (antimony, gold). Supply chain resilience supports semiconductor capex thesis.

factors.name: China Tech & Capital Flows
factors.summary: China NDRC softens foreign investment tone (says no blanket tech ban); but National Team to cut ETF stakes by ~90% in H1 2026, signaling reduced domestic support. DeepSeek raising $10B (70B yuan) for AGI push—new competition for hyperscalers.

factors.name: Credit Market Stress Signals
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports bond market flashing 'termite' warnings: opaque AI loans, excess leverage, credit spreads widening vs equities. When credit and stocks diverge, credit typically correct—warns of earnings/growth deceleration risk.

factors.name: Valuation Warnings
factors.summary: MarketWatch 'Buffett Indicator' warns of market top: grotesque 230% valuation gap, energy crisis risk, stocks running on fumes. Technical sell signals flashing despite Nvidia hype per analysis.

factors.name: Upcoming Holiday & Lower Liquidity
factors.summary: US markets closed Monday May 25 (Memorial Day), bond markets early Friday 2pm ET. Reduced liquidity into holiday weekend historically increases chop and whipsaw risk; limits follow-through on moves.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between conflicting forces: AI capex demand and mega-cap earnings (SoftBank +11%, Lenovo +13%) provide tactical upside, but the Fed's hawkish tilt under Warsh, rising real yield expectations, strong USD, and widening credit stress signals weigh heavily. Japan CPI surprise to downside (1.4% vs 1.7%), softer Eurozone PMIs, and MarketWatch 'Buffett warnings' suggest equities are running on narrative rather than macro fundamentals. The move (+0.88% in 24h) is modest and early-stage, but momentum is limited by Friday close/Memorial Day liquidity drain.
catalysts_ahead: US Michigan Consumer Sentiment (Friday evening after current news snapshot)
Memorial Day US market closure (Monday May 25; equity markets shut, bonds early close Friday 2pm ET)
BOJ rate decision expectations (June meeting per ING, despite soft April CPI)
RBNZ rate decision (May 27; hold expected per Reuters poll, but Q3 hike signaled)
US-Iran ceasefire talks outcome (ongoing; any escalation/breakthrough impacts oil/inflation narrative)
Upcoming mega-cap earnings revisions if macro data continues to soften
Possible Bank of Japan or Fed policy shift signals over next 2-4 weeks
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran ceasefire reaches agreement → oil crash likely, inflation fears ease, real yields fall sharply, and long-duration tech could surge (invalidating bearish bias). Conversely, if Fed maintains 2-3 hikes through 2026 despite soft inflation, and credit markets continue to bleed, equities could gap lower 1-2% on next open (after holiday). Holiday liquidity vacuum could trigger gap/whipsaw either direction.
screening.entry_guidance: HOLD bias until after US Michigan Sentiment release Friday + market reopens Tuesday (post-holiday). If NAS100 holds above 29,200 into close and reopens with conviction on the upside Tuesday, consider small longs with tight stops at 29,100. If breaks below 29,200 with high-volume selling, re-assess bias to SELL below 29,000. Avoid aggressive longs into the holiday given credit warnings and Warsh's hawkish optics; wait for consolidation retest after liquidity returns.

factors.name: AI/Hyperscaler momentum
factors.summary: SoftBank added $61B market cap in 2 days on AI optimism; Lenovo surged 15% on record earnings with AI revenue nearly doubling; Nvidia guidance and hyperscaler cloud capex expectations remain positive tailwind for mega-cap tech.

factors.name: US-Iran peace deal hopes
factors.summary: Secretary of State Rubio signals 'good signs' toward agreement; Asia-Pacific markets rallied on easing geopolitical risk; oil weakness on deal optimism supports growth sentiment and reduces stagflation fears for equity multiples.

factors.name: Real yields / Fed rate expectations
factors.summary: Dollar holds six-week highs on hawkish Fed bets, which pressures long-duration tech multiples; however, oil weakness and Iran deal optimism suggest inflation shock risks receding, potentially capping further rate hike probability.

factors.name: Equity breadth and sector rotation
factors.summary: Global equities near all-time highs; cyclicals leading; EM stocks extended gains; tech sector benefiting from AI trade continuation and reduced geopolitical tail risk.

factors.name: UK/European economic data deterioration
factors.summary: UK retail sales collapsed -1.3% MoM vs -0.6% expected (ONS, May 22); French business climate sank to Feb 2021 lows; German PMI already signals Q2 contraction — war impact on consumer/services spreading.

factors.name: USD strength / DXY
factors.summary: Dollar Index holds near 99.50 on hawkish Fed expectations; stronger USD headwind for NDX multinationals' international earnings translation and EM debt servicing.

factors.name: Nvidia / Semiconductor sentiment
factors.summary: Nvidia earnings report cited as highly anticipated; AI hyperscaler demand commentary front-and-center; no negative supply chain disruptions or China export control shocks in latest news.

factors.name: VIX / volatility regime
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in news; however, options positioning and dealer gamma around mega-caps appear balanced with Iran headline risk moderating.

factors.name: Stagflation risk from geopolitics
factors.summary: Oil prices down 3 days and capped near $96 on Iran deal hopes; however, Strait of Hormuz closure risk remains; Bloomberg survey shows most expect $81–$100 Brent range over 12m, offsetting supply loss with demand destruction.

factors.name: China tech regulation / FDI stance
factors.summary: NDRC softened tone on foreign investment in Chinese tech, denying blanket restrictions; clarified foreign capital must comply with law and national security rules, reducing acute regulatory uncertainty.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in early-to-mid Phase 2 of a relief rally (+0.88% in 24h, +0.11% in 4h) driven by converging bullish catalysts: AI mega-cap earnings momentum (Lenovo +15%, SoftBank +$61B), U.S.-Iran peace deal progress signaling reduced geopolitical tail risk, and moderating oil prices supporting growth sentiment. However, the move is already underway and confidence is capped by (1) hawkish Fed expectations keeping real yields elevated and USD strong, (2) deteriorating UK/European consumer data (UK retail -1.3% MoM) signaling stagflation contagion, and (3) lack of fresh confirmation in the past 4 hours. Entry risk is climbing as NDX approaches intraday resistance; pullback to support would offer better risk/reward.
catalysts_ahead: Nvidia earnings (date TBD but flagged as highly anticipated imminent driver)
German IFO Business Climate survey (flagged as upcoming European session highlight)
U.S. Fed policy and rate expectations updates (hawkish bets still priced in)
Iran-Israel conflict resolution announcement or escalation (headline risk dominant)
CPI/PCE data in next 1–2 weeks (inflation surprise would reprrice growth multiples)
Treasury yield moves on real rates and inflation expectations
Microsoft, Google, Amazon earnings (late May / early June expected)
screening.risk_warning: Move is already +0.88% in 24h and entering resistance — late Phase 2 rally chop and mean reversion pullback is elevated risk. If Strait of Hormuz remains closed or Iran deal collapses, oil could spike >$100, re-igniting stagflation fears and compressing NDX multiples. Hawkish Fed surprise or hotter-than-expected inflation data would invalidate the rally and send real yields higher, pressuring long-duration tech severely. Weak earnings or lowered cloud capex guidance from hyperscalers would break the AI momentum pillar.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in progress (+0.88% 24h); avoid chasing into resistance at 29500–29650. If NAS100 holds 29200–29300 support on intraday pullback, that offers better risk/reward re-entry for continuation. Prefer to wait for retest of the 29100–29200 zone before adding longs — avoid FOMO buying into Phase 2 exhaustion.

factors.name: AI tailwinds & mega-cap earnings
factors.summary: UBS revised S&P 500 target to 7,900 citing resilient US equities despite Middle East headwinds; Bloomberg reports AI stocks driving rally while equal-weighted S&P 500 flat. Lenovo surged ~20% on record earnings with AI revenue nearly doubling (per US Top News).

factors.name: US-Iran peace deal optimism
factors.summary: Secretary of State Rubio signaled 'good signs' for deal; markets priced in relief rally yesterday and today (Dow/S&P 500 futures +0.26-0.34%, NAS100 futures +0.34% per FXStreet). Geopolitical risk premium unwinding supports risk assets.

factors.name: Real yields & Fed policy uncertainty
factors.summary: ECB policymaker Demarco signals probable June hike; Fed hawkish outlook supported by resilient US data (per FXStreet). Rising real yields typically compress NDX multiples; however, move is still early, not yet fully priced.

factors.name: Euro weakness & DXY strength
factors.summary: EUR/USD easing toward 1.1600 on geopolitical uncertainty (FXStreet); USD/JPY edging toward April peak 160.50 on hawkish Fed. Stronger dollar headwind for NDX given high international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Crude oil volatility capped
factors.summary: Brent supported by supply shock risk but US-Iran deal progress limits upside (ING/Rabobank); gold near $4,500 with limited directional conviction. Energy inflation concern reduced but not eliminated.

factors.name: Tech sentiment & flow data
factors.summary: SoftBank Group added $61B+ market cap in 2 days on AI optimism; XRP ETFs attracting inflows while Bitcoin/Ether funds struggle, showing selective risk-on in growth assets. Analyst calls increasingly bullish post-earnings.

factors.name: China regulatory overhang
factors.summary: China cracking down on illegal cross-border trading; Futu Holdings penalized for unlicensed mainland operations (Bloomberg). Adds structural headwind for China-exposed tech names and fintech platforms.

factors.name: Mega-IPO window (SpaceX June 12, OpenAI pending)
factors.summary: Analysts flag mega-IPO cycle could signal market top, but also indicates sustained institutional appetite for growth/tech. SpaceX IPO largest in history signals risk-on environment.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in early Phase 2 of a recovery rally driven by (1) US-Iran peace deal optimism reducing geopolitical risk premium, (2) mega-cap tech earnings resilience and AI capex enthusiasm (UBS upgrade, Lenovo surge), and (3) selective institutional appetite for growth. However, headwinds include rising real yields on ECB/Fed hawkish signals, USD strength pressuring international revenues, and China regulatory crackdown on fintech. The move has already run +0.34% in NAS100 futures overnight, so immediate upside is not extended but buyers are testing resistance near 29,550.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal announcement or collapse (hours–days; binary risk)
ECB June 11 meeting expectations (rate hike likelihood climbing to 88%, per Investinglive)
Fed speakers and rate-hike expectations; next FOMC (likely late June)
Tesla, Microsoft, Google, Apple, Amazon earnings (if not yet priced)
SpaceX IPO launch (June 12 preliminary; IPO roadshow timing)
CPI/PCE data in June (inflation recalibration risk)
Nvidia earnings & guidance on H100/H200 demand, China headwinds
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran talks collapse or escalate, geopolitical bid reverses sharply (NAS100 -1 to -2% intraday). If Fed signals aggressive tightening (e.g. FOMC hawkish surprise), real yields spike and long-duration growth multiples compress, invalidating BUY thesis. China regulatory clampdown on ADRs/fintech could spook flows. Move is already +0.34% in 4h, so if resistance at 29,550 fails to hold decisively, pullback to 29,250–29,350 likely before next leg.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 with +0.34% gain already posted; wait for retest of 29,300–29,350 support or a clean break above 29,550 resistance on volume before chasing. Geopolitical event risk remains binary, so reduce size on entries until Iran deal clarity emerges.

factors.name: S&P 500 earnings momentum
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports S&P 500 on track for strongest earnings growth since 2021, with momentum spreading beyond AI to broader corporate America. Nasdaq 100 (heavily weighted to earnings drivers) benefits directly.

factors.name: AI capex enthusiasm
factors.summary: Nvidia announced $18.6B venture investments in 3 months, hyperscalers remain bullish. Workday shares jumped on AI demand easing investor concerns. No negative capex guidance from major cloud players in news window.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech buyer sentiment
factors.summary: Nvidia mega buyback announcement, multiple analyst reiterations (BMO on Workday, Take-Two, Walmart). Tom Lee says trillions in tech IPO supply won't crash S&P 500, suggesting structural support.

factors.name: Treasury yields / real yields
factors.summary: Yields sliding modestly (stock futures rising amid falling Treasury yields per 13:02 report), but 2-year and 10-year remain elevated with Fed tightening still priced. Kevin Warsh taking over Fed Chair today—early messaging likely hawkish or non-committal on cuts.

factors.name: Fed policy transition
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed Chair today at White House ceremony. Rabobank notes Warsh may avoid pushing immediate rate cuts, laying groundwork for later easing—introduces policy uncertainty. No firm near-term rate cut signal.

factors.name: Geopolitical / oil risk
factors.summary: US-Iran peace talks show 'slight progress' per Secretary Rubio; Qatar negotiators in Tehran. Oil fell to $95.46, now $96.10 (below swing area $96.34–$97.34). Lower oil reduces margin compression risk for NDX, but fragile optimism not guaranteed resolution.

factors.name: Inflation data (Canada, Mexico)
factors.summary: Canada PPI +2.0% m/m vs +1.3% expected (hotter), but Mexico mid-May inflation slowed. Mixed inflation prints don't support aggressive Fed easing; real yields remain sticky.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD marginally higher to start session; carries support from elevated yields. Weaker USD would typically help NDX multinationals; strong USD is modest headwind but not dominant driver today.

factors.name: Sector rotation / tech breadth
factors.summary: S&P 500 on track for eighth consecutive weekly gain. Stocks poised to extend yesterday's gains. Tech leadership holding despite mixed macro; NDX benefiting from secular AI bid.

factors.name: Insider selling / sentiment risks
factors.summary: Trump Media moved another $205M in Bitcoin, losses on crypto bet now $455M. Mark Cuban sells Bitcoin. BofA's Hartnett warns mega-IPOs (SpaceX, OpenAI) risk bubble-like concentration in tech benchmarks. Subtle warning of froth.

factors.name: Weekly momentum / chart setup
factors.summary: Nasdaq futures up 0.2% ahead of holiday weekend. Trump tweeted 'NEW STOCK MARKET RECORD!' Dow near February record; NDX/SPX have more work to do but technicals support continuation into month-end.

factors.name: Volatility & positioning
factors.summary: No major VIX/VXN spike; Goldman Sachs highlighting hedging on bullish bets suggests complacency. Options market not flashing extreme bullish gamma; late-day Friday chop likely.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 enters Friday supported by accelerating S&P 500 earnings momentum, relentless AI capex enthusiasm (Nvidia, hyperscalers), and broad-based mega-cap buyer strength. Kevin Warsh's Fed swearing-in today introduces policy uncertainty—early messaging will matter, but market bias remains constructive pending explicit hawkish signals. Geopolitical risk (US-Iran talks) is easing, oil down to near $96, reducing inflation tail risk. However, real yields remain elevated due to sticky inflation expectations (Canada PPI hot), and BofA warns of concentration bubble in mega-cap tech ahead of mega-IPOs. Market is in PHASE_2: move in progress (8 consecutive weekly gains), so late chasers should wait for consolidation.
catalysts_ahead: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair first policy message / press conference (today, May 22)
US-Iran ceasefire negotiations update (fluid, could resolve or deteriorate into weekend)
Oil inventory data / geopolitical headlines (early next week)
Memorial Day market close (markets close early Monday May 26 US)
Next FOMC meeting (early June) — Warsh's first decision point
screening.risk_warning: Invalidation: (1) Hot CPI/PCE print early next week → triggers real-yield spike, pressures long-duration growth multiples. (2) Warsh signals hawkish 'patient easing' delay → growth stock multiple compression. (3) US-Iran talks break down sharply → oil spikes >$105, inflation fears resurface, margin pressure on NDX. (4) Mega-cap earnings misses or guidance cuts on capex slowdown. (5) Tech concentration bubble narrative accelerates if OpenAI/SpaceX IPO details emerge as aggressive valuations.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress (8 consecutive weekly gains); wait for Friday close consolidation or early next week pullback toward 20,300 support before fresh BUY. If Warsh's remarks are dovish, re-rate higher. Late Friday chop expected into holiday weekend.

factors.name: Fed Chair Warsh Hawkish Positioning
factors.summary: Bond traders fully pricing in rate hike by December 2026 per Bloomberg. Fed Governor Waller stated should 'remove easing bias' and does not expect near-term rate cuts; outcome depends on Iran conflict length. This hawkish pivot pressures long-duration growth multiples.

factors.name: Real Yields & Inflation Expectations Rising
factors.summary: UMich May final sentiment at 44.8 vs 48.2 expected; 1-year inflation expectations jumped to 4.8% vs 4.6% prior, 5-year at 3.9% vs 3.4%. Rising real yields compression multi-multiple for Nasdaq's high-beta, long-duration names.

factors.name: Oil & Geopolitical Risk (Iran War)
factors.summary: US-Iran conflict continues; oil peaked $96-97 range; consumer sentiment hit all-time low on inflation/war fears per UMich report. Supply disruptions in Strait of Hormuz elevate stagflation risk and real rate pressures.

factors.name: Tech Sector Selective Strength
factors.summary: Semiconductors leading: AMD +4.25%, TXN +3.94%, INTC +2.73% per market analysis. Workday +5% on AI margin strength. NVDA -1.45% shows mixed sentiment, but narrow semicon strength could support NDX near-term.

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment Collapse
factors.summary: Consumer sentiment fell for third consecutive month to all-time lows; K-shaped economy emerging (wealthy resilient, lower-income struggling). Weak retail backdrop contradicts typical growth multiple support.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Risk-Off Bias
factors.summary: USD recovering on Warsh hawkish signals and Iran deal delay uncertainty. USDCAD above 200-day MA; broad dollar strength typically headwinds for US equities with international revenue (Nasdaq heavy).

factors.name: Early-Week Rally Momentum
factors.summary: Bias history shows three consecutive BUY 6/10 calls (07:43, 10:50, 13:32 on 2026-05-22). Dow hitting new all-time highs; NDX +152pts at start of session reflects some risk-on momentum despite headwinds.

factors.name: Credit & Leverage Concerns
factors.summary: JPMorgan CEO warned of 'cockroaches' in credit markets; MarketWatch reports 'termite' infestation (opaque AI loans, excessive leverage) hollowing economy. Private credit stress parallels 2023 SVB run, raising tail risk.

factors.name: Upcoming AI/Tech Catalysts Mixed
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO expected (could boost Mag 7 to Mag 8 weighting); GitHub outages hurt Copilot momentum; Congressional probes into prediction markets add regulatory uncertainty. Conflicting directional signals.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Surge & Growth Multiple Compression
factors.summary: 10-year yields surging on hawkish Fed repricing; real yields rising sharply compress Nasdaq's long-duration valuations. No meaningful correction yet, but structural headwind for growth stocks.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq staged a modest early-session rally (Dow +340pts, NDX +152pts) on hope for Iran peace deal, but underlying pressure is mounting: Warsh's Fed is now explicitly hawkish (rate hike fully priced by Dec), UMich sentiment collapsed to all-time low, and real yields are rising sharply on elevated inflation expectations (1Y at 4.8%). Semiconductors leading (+3-4%) provides tactical support, but broad consumer weakness, credit stress warnings, and dollar strength argue against sustained risk-on. Move is already underway (Phase 2); initial rally may face retest and pullback into near-term resistance.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting (date TBD, likely late June 2026)
PCE inflation release (to confirm/deny hawkish repricing)
NFP (monthly, late May/early June)
Mega-cap earnings season (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, META, AMZN guidance on capex/AI demand)
Iran war update / oil supply shock risk (daily)
SpaceX IPO (timing not specified, adds Mag 8 tail risk)
UK/Eurozone inflation data (May CPI in coming days)
screening.risk_warning: Bias would flip to SELL if: (1) Oil drops sharply on Iran peace deal breakthrough, killing inflation premium and allowing dovish pivot; (2) Major tech earnings or capex guidance disappoints; (3) Real yields reverse lower on soft inflation data; (4) VXN spikes on credit stress / tail-risk event (SVB-style run on private credit). Conversely, a hawkish pause from Warsh or surprise soft inflation print could spark fresh BUY momentum.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (early rally underway); wait for retest of intraday lows (~19,300) or consolidation above 19,500 before adding long. If rally extends >1% without pullback, consider waiting for 19,800 resistance rejection and new support form before chasing.

factors.name: Tech earnings momentum
factors.summary: Zoom raised FY guidance beating estimates, Workday shares jumped on AI demand ease (Bloomberg 18:00-17:42). Nvidia, Walmart, Nike reporting Monday ahead; typically strong earnings cycles support NDX.

factors.name: AI capex and semiconductor demand
factors.summary: Continued hyperscaler capex optimism evident in Zoom/Workday upgrades and AI token strength (NEAR up 50% on AI momentum per CoinDesk 17:20). No supply-chain disruptions flagged; TSMC/ASML stable.

factors.name: Fed policy uncertainty and Warsh swearing-in
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh sworn in as 17th Fed Chair on 2026-05-22 (Bloomberg 17:58, 16:56). Trump publicly told him to 'be independent' and 'do your own thing' (16:15, 16:03). Market initially positive (Dow hit ATH), but Waller's hawkish inflation comments (14:54 feed) and talk of potential rate hikes by end-of-year create medium-term headwind for duration.

factors.name: Real yields and long-duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Gold slipped on Waller's hawkish tone lifting USD and pricing in potential Fed hike (FXStreet 18:10). Elevated real yields pressure high-multiple growth stocks; 10Y Treasury at elevated levels (precise level not stated, but commentary indicates upward pressure).

factors.name: Macro headwinds: consumer sentiment, inflation expectations
factors.summary: Consumer sentiment hit all-time low in May amid Iran war inflation fears (US Top News 16:13). University of Michigan inflation expectations moved higher, supporting US yields higher (Investinglive 14:46). CPI/inflation surprises not yet released but expectation shift is hawkish.

factors.name: Iran ceasefire optimism and oil/energy dynamics
factors.summary: Sky News Arabia reports negotiations in Tehran reached understanding on nuclear issue (Investinglive 17:38); WSJ reports mediators rushing to prevent military action (16:50). Oil rig count up most in 4 years (18:24), crude near $96.35, below 100/200h MA, suggesting energy volatility containment. Cautious USD support from geopolitical premium, but risk-on if deal solidifies.

factors.name: Dollar strength and currency backdrop
factors.summary: USD ending week strong on Fed rate expectations and contained risk sentiment (Scotiabank via FXStreet 15:03, Bloomberg 18:35). Dollar strength typically headwind for international NDX revenue, but near-term technicals favor USD.

factors.name: Antitrust and regulatory risk
factors.summary: Google appeals US court ruling on search monopoly (All News 18:24). SEC delays crypto ETFs and tokenized stock exemptions (18:33, 17:30). No new mega-cap enforcement, but regulatory friction present.

factors.name: Short-term price action
factors.summary: 4h change -0.02%, 24h change +0.33%. Minimal intra-day momentum. Dow hit ATH Friday despite hawkish Warsh swearing-in, suggesting some resilience, but NQ lagging with NVDA down 1.45% and semiconductors mixed.

factors.name: Bias history continuity
factors.summary: Three consecutive BUY6/10 reads (10:50, 13:32, 16:26 on 2026-05-22), none triggered confirmation. Today's read remains aligned but lacks fresh catalyst confirmation; this is a continuation bias, not a new reaction.

key_levels.resistance: 29800–30000 (all-time high region implied by Dow ATH narrative)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between two competing narratives: (1) strong tech earnings (Zoom, Workday) and sustained AI capex demand (Hyperscaler optimism), and (2) an increasingly hawkish Fed under new Chair Warsh, elevated real yields, and consumer sentiment collapsed to all-time lows due to Iran war inflation fears. The Dow hit all-time highs Friday despite Warsh's swearing-in, suggesting some de-risking away from long-duration growth toward value. Iran ceasefire talks offer modest relief, but persistent oil/energy price support and inflation expectations rising limit downside. NQ has stalled at current levels with minimal 4h momentum, implying a period of consolidation rather than breakout. Catalyst density is high Monday (earnings from Nvidia, Walmart, Nike) and next week (potential FOMC-relevant data), so conviction is capped.
catalysts_ahead: Nvidia, Walmart, Nike earnings Monday (2026-05-26 est.)
Microsoft, Apple, Google, Amazon earnings next week
US inflation data (CPI/PCE) — timing TBD, typically mid-week
Iran ceasefire negotiations update — geopolitical driver
Fed speakers (Warsh, Waller, other governors) — rate expectations
Treasury yield moves (10Y critical for NQ multiples)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran talks collapse and crude spikes >$105, or if CPI prints hot (>0.4% MoM), real yields surge, and Warsh signals hawkish tilt, NDX could break down toward 29000–28500. Conversely, if earnings beats are strong and Fed signals patience, move extends toward 30000. Warsh's 'reform-oriented' Fed remains opaque on timeline and magnitude — policy surprise risk is elevated. Monday earnings will be a key retest point; if mega-caps disappoint guidance, confidence flips to SELL.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 consolidation after 3 consecutive BUY6/10 reads with no confirmation. Wait for Monday earnings catalysts (NVDA, WMT, NKE) to confirm directional bias before chasing. If NDX holds above 29400 support and earnings beat, consider long entry on breakout above 29800. If earnings disappoint or Iran talks fail, expect retest toward 29000–29200 for better risk/reward.

factors.name: Fed Chair Warsh Sworn In (Hawkish Mandate)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh took oath as 17th Fed Chair on 2026-05-22; Bloomberg describes him as 'most hawkish Fed nominee in 20 years' (Sonal Desai, Franklin Templeton). His reform-oriented stance and promise to reshape Fed operations creates near-term uncertainty for growth multiples sensitive to real rates.

factors.name: Long-Bond Yield Spike & Real Rates Pressure
factors.summary: Global long-bond yields hit highest levels in ~2 decades (per Bloomberg 2026-05-22T19:18); Subadra Rajappa (Société Générale) warns move 'has room to run.' Rising real yields directly compress Nasdaq 100's duration-heavy mega-cap valuations (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, TSLA).

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Talks & Oil/Geopolitical Risk-Off
factors.summary: Sky News Arabia reports 'understanding on broad outlines regarding nuclear issue' (2026-05-22T17:38); crude oil fell to new lows. De-risking of oil shock removes inflation-tail-risk that would force Warsh into aggressive hiking, slightly easing growth-multiple pressure.

factors.name: AI Momentum & Microsoft/Zoom Strength
factors.summary: Microsoft's Fara1.5 AI agents beat OpenAI/Google on web browsing (2026-05-22T20:31); Zoom raised FY forecast and jumped on earnings (2026-05-22T18:01). Hyperscaler AI capex narrative remains intact; cloud demand supports mega-cap tech guidance.

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment At Record Lows
factors.summary: US consumer sentiment hit fresh record low in May amid Iran war and inflation worries (2026-05-22T16:13). Weakness in lower-income cohorts and K-shaped retail split suggest macro headwinds could weigh on growth expectations and earnings guidance.

factors.name: 8-Week Win Streak; Dow at All-Time High
factors.summary: Wall Street posts 8-week winning streak, longest since Dec 2023 (2026-05-22T20:11); Dow hit record close (+0.58%, 2026-05-22T20:30). Price action shows momentum, but NAS100 is +0.23% on day and only -0.58% 4h—move is modest and not extended.

factors.name: Valuation Concerns & 'Buffett Indicator' Warnings
factors.summary: MarketWatch warns '230% valuation gap' and market 'running on fumes' (2026-05-22T20:32). With Warsh's hawkish stance and bond yields spiking, multiple compression risk is elevated for extended-duration tech names.

factors.name: Crypto/Risk Sentiment Soft
factors.summary: Bitcoin sold off to $76k (Cointelegraph 5/22); Trump Media liquidating 2,650 BTC (~$205M) to Crypto.com. Risk-off tilt in alternatives reflects unease despite equity indices holding near highs; correlation breakdowns suggest fragile positioning.

factors.name: Regulatory Headwinds (SEC, Antitrust, Prediction Markets)
factors.summary: SEC delays crypto tokenized stock exemption (2026-05-22T18:33); Congressional probes into Kalshi/Polymarket insider trading underway. No immediate mega-cap hits, but regulatory uncertainty adds friction to sentiment.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 faces a critical inflection. Kevin Warsh's hawkish Fed chair debut, combined with a spike in long-bond yields to 2-decade highs, creates near-term headwind for duration-heavy mega-caps. However, US-Iran peace talks have eased oil/geopolitical risk, and AI momentum (MSFT, ZOOM, hyperscaler capex) remains a countervailing bullish narrative. The 8-week win streak and Dow record suggest residual bullish sentiment, but the -0.58% 4-hour move and record-low consumer confidence indicate the market is entering a consolidation or retest phase. Warsh's inaugural speech emphasizes 'reform' and learning from past Fed mistakes—code for potential tightening bias—which conflicts with growth multiples priced for lower-for-longer real rates.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC minutes or hawkish guidance from other Fed speakers (expect rate-hike chatter to persist)
Next CPI/PCE report (timing TBD; could trigger yields higher and NAS100 lower if hot)
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL earnings guidance (AI capex commentary is critical for NDX)
10-year Treasury yield continuation above 4.5% (structural break; watch for further real-rate shock)
Oil price stability / further Iran peace progress (risk-on if confirmed; risk-off if talks collapse)
Mega-cap earnings revisions in response to higher-for-longer rates
screening.risk_warning: Bias assumes Warsh's hawkish rhetoric does not trigger a rapid >1.5% drop in NAS100 or a 50bp spike in 10-year yield. If either occurs, long-duration growth names will face acute selling. Conversely, if Iran deal is finalized and inflation expectations drop sharply, real yields could compress and support a retest of recent highs. A major earnings miss from NVDA or MSFT would instantly flip bias to SELL.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (underway but not extended). Do not chase long positions here; wait for a retest of 29300–29400 support on any dip to build conviction. If Nasdaq breaks below 29250 on a Warsh hawkish headline or bond yield spike >4.6%, consider that a capitulation point for a deeper leg down. On the upside, resistance at 29600–29750 is critical; break above that on strong close + positive Fed signals could signal PHASE 1 continuation to new highs.

factors.name: New Fed Chair Warsh (Hawkish Surprise)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh sworn in as 17th Fed Chair on Friday; described as 'most hawkish Fed nominee in 20 years' by Franklin Templeton CIO. Warsh signaled 'reform-oriented Fed' and focus on inflation control, which pressures long-duration growth multiples and Nasdaq 100 components.

factors.name: Real Yields & Long Bond Selloff
factors.summary: Global long-bond yields hit highest in nearly two decades per Bloomberg; Société Générale warns move 'has room to run.' Higher real yields compress valuations on high-multiple tech/growth stocks that dominate NAS100.

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment Collapse
factors.summary: Consumer sentiment tumbled to fresh record low in May amid Iran war and oil-price inflation fears. Negative sentiment typically precedes weakness in discretionary spending, weighing on mega-cap consumer-facing tech (AMZN, TSLA).

factors.name: US-Iran Negotiations (Risk-Off / Risk-On Whipsaw)
factors.summary: Modest progress reported in Tehran talks (nuclear agreement outline, Strait of Hormuz negotiations ongoing), creating intraday volatility. Oil fell from highs but remains elevated (~$96.35). Uncertainty keeps risk sentiment choppy; no major resolution imminent.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings & AI Capex Narrative
factors.summary: Zoom raised FY guidance (sales and EPS), triggering upgrades; Qualcomm rallying on AI device boom narrative; Microsoft's Fara1.5 open-weight browser agents beat OpenAI/Google. Hyperscaler capex and semiconductor demand remain constructive longer-term.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness & Risk Appetite (Intraday)
factors.summary: Dollar ended week nearly unchanged; risk assets got modest boost from Iran peace hopes. Weaker dollar typically supports NAS100 international revenue exposure (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL).

factors.name: VIX / Volatility Environment
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in news window; implied volatility likely elevated due to geopolitical uncertainty (Iran) and new hawkish Fed regime. Elevated vol typically pressures long-duration growth plays.

factors.name: Regulatory Headwinds (SEC, FTC, Antitrust)
factors.summary: SEC delaying crypto-tokenized stock innovation exemption; Congress probing insider trading on prediction markets (Kalshi, Polymarket). Accumulating regulatory friction on fintech/crypto, though not directly hitting mega-cap tech earnings yet.

factors.name: Price Action: 4h -0.58%, 24h -0.02%
factors.summary: Nasdaq 100 flat to slightly down over last 24h; modest 4h decline suggests intraday weakness but no directional conviction. Move NOT extended; still early in session structure.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between two competing narratives: hawkish new Fed Chair Warsh (sworn in Friday) and higher real yields pressing valuations, versus constructive mega-cap tech earnings (Zoom, Qualcomm) and AI capex demand. Geopolitical uncertainty (Iran talks) and collapsing consumer sentiment add chop. The 4h decline of -0.58% and flat 24h action suggest the market is digesting the Warsh regime change intraday but has not yet decisively repriced. Real yields remain the critical headwind for long-duration growth multiples.
catalysts_ahead: Monday opening: market reaction to Warsh's first full trading week as Fed Chair
US CPI / Core CPI (if scheduled within 72h; typically high-impact for NAS100 repricing)
FOMC minutes or Warsh commentary on monetary policy path
Mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN dates TBD)
Iran negotiations resolution or escalation (oil price feedback loop)
Treasury yield action (10Y, real yields) — tracking closely
screening.risk_warning: Bullish case invalidates if: (1) real yields spike further on hawkish FOMC commentary, (2) consumer spending data disappoints, (3) Iran escalates and oil spikes >$110, (4) mega-cap tech guidance misses. Bearish case invalidates if: (1) Iran deal agreed (risk-on, equity relief), (2) Fed holds steady and allows yields to stabilize, (3) mega-cap earnings surprise to upside with strong guidance.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (intraday weakness, not extended). Wait for NAS100 retest of 29600 resistance or hold of 29300 support before committing. Do not chase the 4h decline; better entry on pullback or confirmation of Fed-driven repricing on Monday open.
